name: Manabí (Ecuador)
description: Five equal horizontal stripes alternating green and white, beginning and ending with green; a red isosceles triangle along the hoist with its apex at the centre of the flag, charged with five white five-pointed stars.
history: Manabí Province was established on 25 June 1824; its capital is Portoviejo and its largest city Manta, a major Pacific port. The pattern recalls the Cuban national flag, rendered in Manabí's green and white.

features.role: red isosceles triangle along the hoist with its apex at the centre of the flag
five white five-pointed stars arranged on the red hoist triangle
